step_by_step_thinking: Synarthroses refer to joints that are immovable and consist primarily of fibrous connective tissue. Based on the definitions given, both sutures and syndesmoses fit under the category of synarthroses because they involve bone connections through dense fibrous tissues without allowing any substantial range of motion. Additionally, gomphosis describes the tooth-to-alveolus attachment within the jawbone, another form of immobile articulation via fibrous tissue. Therefore, all three mentioned examples fall under the definition of synarthroses.
raw_response:
Synarthroses refer to joints that are immovable and consist primarily of fibrous connective tissue. Based on the definitions given, both sutures and syndesmoses fit under the category of synarthroses because they involve bone connections through dense fibrous tissues without allowing any substantial range of motion. Additionally, gomphosis describes the tooth-to-alveolus attachment within the jawbone, another form of immobile articulation via fibrous tissue. Therefore, all three mentioned examples fall under the definition of synarthroses.
\boxed{D} Given my explanation about how each listed option fits the definition of synarthroses — being immovable joints composed mainly of fibrous connective tissue — D (\boxed{\text{All of the above}}) accurately encapsulates them all. Thus, the comprehensive nature of "all" makes D the correct answer according to the criteria set forth by the available information.
